entity.description: Junction table connecting domains to the data objects they hold, with a mastery qualifier. Captures the observation that Customer lives in CRM (primary), Billing (secondary), and Support (secondary), the seed of the data-silo map.

computed_fields.name: domain_data_object_label
computed_fields.description: Composed from the parent domain name and the parent data object name. Recomputed on every write so renames in either parent flow through.

fields.title: Role
fields.description: The domain's role with respect to this data object. master: authoritative owner of some slice in a holistic deployment. embedded_master: point-solution local master — owns its own copy when deployed standalone; demoted to consumer at runtime when a canonical master is also deployed in the same environment. contributor: writes some fields without being authoritative. consumer: reads only. derived: produces computed projections only. Combined with the necessity column, embedded_master+required rows count toward the Signal 1 multi-master integration-burden score; embedded_master+optional rows do not.

fields.title: Necessity
fields.description: Whether the domain's relationship to this data object is load-bearing for its workflow. required: the domain cannot function in any realistic deployment without this relationship being active (e.g. OMS reads customers, ATS embedded-masters positions). optional: the domain tolerates absence — the read/write happens only in some deployment configurations or for some workflows (e.g. ATS embedded-masters cost_centers when finance isn't deployed alongside, but functions fine without). Combined with role, embedded_master+required rows count toward the Signal 1 multi-master integration-burden score; embedded_master+optional rows do not.

Comp / withholding / payable slice — gross-to-net rules, deductions, tax setup, bank account, pay history. HCM masters the core HR record, Payroll masters the payable view, IGA masters the identity view.
PAYROLL local-masters positions for pay-grade / FLSA classification / GL-allocation tagging when no HCM is deployed; reads from HCM when present.

PAYROLL consumes benefit_enrollments to compute pre/post-tax benefit deductions per pay_cycle.
PAYROLL consumes benefit_dependents only when imputed-income or dependent-coverage tax treatment is in scope (US health plans, life-insurance >50k). Many deployments skip this.
PAYROLL consumes approved merit_recommendations as the source of pay-rate changes effective the next pay_cycle.
PAYROLL consumes approved time_entries from WFM as the source of variable-pay hours (regular, overtime, premium, on-call) per pay_cycle.
PAYROLL consumes approved absence_requests as paid-leave hours feeding the pay cycle.

Recruiting execution — ATS masters the pipeline-stage, candidate, interview, offer, and acceptance slice. SWP co-masters the headcount-intent slice (position approval, budget alignment, plan-to-actual). Cross-domain handoff SWP→ATS on headcount.approved is the bridge.

LMS local-masters job_profiles for competency-to-course mapping in skills-cloud LMS deployments; many LMS deployments skip job_profile binding.
LMS local-masters org_units for department-scoped course assignment, manager hierarchy, and team-level reporting when deployed standalone.
LMS local-masters cost_centers for training-cost allocation only when finance integration exists or training-budget tracking is enabled; many deployments skip cost-centre tagging on training.
Writes talent-specific fields (career aspirations, mobility preferences, succession status) but does not master the core HR record.
TALENT-MGMT local-masters positions for succession-planning, internal-mobility, and nine-box grouping when deployed standalone.
Contributes the skills/competencies slice — the required capability profile for the role, used in succession and internal-mobility matching. HCM masters the operational profile.
TALENT-MGMT local-masters org_units for manager-hierarchy roll-ups, calibration scoping, and goal-cascade trees when deployed standalone.
TALENT-MGMT contributes performance-derived skill signals (calibrated ratings, succession assessments) into the skill_profile aggregate.
Individual employee OKRs as the goal-setting half of the performance-management cycle, tied to compensation and calibration.
WFM consumes employees as the schedulable resource pool — identity, employment status, and base FTE drive shift eligibility.
WFM consumes positions to enforce position-based scheduling rules: required skills, location, cost-centre, and FTE budget per position.
WFM consumes job_profiles for skill/qualification matching when filling open shifts and for labour-law rules tied to job class.
WFM consumes org_units (locations, departments) as the scheduling hierarchy — schedules are published per org_unit.
WFM local-masters cost_centers for labor-cost allocation when finance integration is absent (retail, restaurant, healthcare deployments often tag shifts by cost_center).
COMP-MGMT writes compensation-state fields (current base, bonus target, equity guideline, salary band placement) on the worker record.
COMP-MGMT local-masters positions for salary-band assignment, job-architecture binding, and pay-equity grouping when no HCM is integrated.
COMP-MGMT local-masters job_profiles for job-level and grade structures when deployed standalone; reads from HCM when integrated.
Compensation planning rolls up through the org tree (manager -> team -> dept -> BU); COMP-MGMT local-masters when standalone.
COMP-MGMT consumes the approved financial_plan to validate merit-cycle budget envelope. Many deployments run comp planning standalone with an Excel-imported budget number.
COMP-MGMT defines compensation-driven earning codes (bonus, equity vest, allowance) that PAYROLL operationalises.
COMP-MGMT consumes calibrated performance_reviews ratings as the primary input to merit_recommendations during cycle planning.
COMP-MGMT local-masters cost_centers for merit-budget allocation only in deployments without finance integration.
Geo-differentiated comp ranges key on work location; optional in single-geography orgs.
BEN-ADMIN writes the benefit-eligibility slice (coverage tier, dependent linkage, beneficiary designation) on the worker record.
BEN-ADMIN local-masters org_units for benefit-group assignment (different benefit eligibility by location, business unit) when no HCM is integrated.
BEN-ADMIN defines the benefit deduction codes that PAYROLL operationalises in gross-to-net calculation.
BEN-ADMIN consumes legal_entities to scope benefit-plan offerings (different plans by employer-of-record / country).
EMP-EXP local-masters the worker record for respondent identity, demographic-cohort breakdowns, and manager-hierarchy filtering when deployed standalone; reads from HCM when integrated.
EMP-EXP local-masters org_units for team-level / department-level engagement breakdowns when deployed standalone.
Read-only — derives KPIs and cohorts from the canonical record. Does not author any slice.
PA reads positions for span-of-control, vacancy-rate, and headcount analytics. Not derived — used as a dimensional source, not republished.
PA reads org_units as the analytical hierarchy for every people-KPI dashboard cut.
Reads the event stream to compute time-based metrics (tenure, time-in-level, time-to-promotion).
Tool-adoption metrics roll into people analytics for productivity and engagement dashboards.
Where customer-related people-analytics overlap exists (employees acting as customers, customer-success-team workload analytics).
Aggregated call quality, talk-ratio, and topic-coverage metrics feed sales-manager coaching analytics in people analytics.
Pay-equity analytics and total-rewards reporting derive from aggregated pay_slip data.
PA reads benefit_enrollments for total-rewards-analytics and benefits-utilisation reporting. Many PA deployments skip benefits analytics.
PA reads compensation_plans for pay-equity analytics that incorporate plan-design context. Many PA deployments skip this.
PA reads merit_recommendations to inform attrition modelling (under-promoted, under-merited workers).
PA reads time_entries for productivity, overtime-burden, and burnout-risk feature engineering.
PA derives leave-trend, burnout-risk, and absenteeism metrics from absence_requests aggregated over time.
PA reads course_enrollments to model learning-engagement features alongside the existing learning_records consumption.
PA consumes aggregated learning_records for L&D ROI, skill-gap closure, and compliance-completion analytics.
PA consumes calibrated performance_reviews for rater-bias analysis, distribution analytics, and high-potential cohort tracking.
PA reads survey_responses (anonymised) to fuse engagement signals into attrition and performance modelling. Distinct from engagement_drivers (aggregate) — this is row-level.
PA consumes engagement_drivers at team/department grain for cross-cutting people-analytics dashboards and attrition modelling features.
ERP-FIN consumes payroll_journal_entries from pay_runs to post personnel cost to the GL.
ERP-FIN accrues employer benefit cost based on active benefit_enrollments.
ERP-FIN masters the canonical cost-center hierarchy; HCM and EPM contribute org-alignment and budget-mapping slices respectively.
Financial-asset master; distinct from operational hardware_assets in ITAM/CMDB.
ERP-FIN consumes approved expense_reports for journal entry posting and reimbursement routing.
ERP-FIN consumes project codes for cost allocation, billing, and rev-rec.
Cost-center alignment slice. EPM contributes the financial mapping that org_units need to support variance reporting. (A future Finance/GL domain would master cost_centers proper.)
EPM consumes period-actuals from ERP-FIN for variance reporting against budgets / forecasts.
Expense submission, policy enforcement, and approval routing all key on the employee's department.
Corporate-card SaaS charges are matched against subscriptions to reconcile expensed-vs-procured SaaS.
When the procurement path overlaps with corporate-card spend, EXPENSE reconciles the duplicate paths.
Reconciliation of bank-issued and corporate-issued cards into expense reports (legacy stack model — txn data sourced from issuer after settlement, not real-time at authorization).
Card-feed ingestion from bank/issuer and reconciliation to expense lines at expense-report submission time (legacy stack; not real-time).

PSA consumes job_profiles for skill-based staffing — matching project skill requirements to consultant qualifications.
PSA consumes signed SOWs (CLM-mastered) to seed project scope, billing rules, and milestone definitions.
PSA consumes won opportunities (Closed Won) as the trigger to spin up a delivery project and seed staffing demand.
PSA contributes project-level rev-rec records under ASC 606 percentage-of-completion or milestone billing; ERP-FIN consolidates into the books.
PSA consumes project-tagged time_entries for billable-hours calculation and utilisation reporting.
PSA consumes project-tagged expense_reports for T&E billing rollup and project-cost reporting.
Services orgs that run delivery in WORK-MGMT feed project completion + utilization into PSA for billing and resource forecasting.

Deal-context overlay: competitor mentions, sentiment shifts, commitments visible on the opportunity timeline.

Reads SUB-MGMT customer invoices for AR reconciliation on recurring orders
